title: Conjecture 5 (Aboulker et al.)
statement_text: If $C$ is an orientation of $C_\ell$, then $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(C) = \ell$.
context_text: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé initiated the study of subdivisions in digraphs of large dichromatic number and conjectured that the dichromatic Mader number of any orientation of an $\ell$-cycle equals $\ell$, which is the smallest it could possibly be. In [1], the conjecture was proved for directed cycles and an upper bound of $2\ell-1$ was established for arbitrary orientations.
attributed_to: Aboulker, Cohen, Havet, Lochet, Moura, and Thomassé
paper_contribution: The paper fully resolves the conjecture: Theorem 7 proves $\mathrm{mader}_{\vec{\chi}}(F)=v(F)$ for every octus digraph $F$, and Corollary 8 specialises this to every orientation of a cactus graph, which in particular covers all orientations of cycles.
